seo.title: Building Voice Agents with DSPy + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with DSPy + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: DSPy enables programming voice agents by composing optimizable LM modules for speech-to-text, reasoning, and text-to-speech pipelines. Guardrails are essential due to real-time latency demands and risks like incoherent conversations or unsafe responses from optimized prompts. Vex provides runtime verification to catch assertion violations and ensure sub-second performance.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with DSPy
architectureOverview.description:
Voice agents process real-time audio through a pipeline: speech-to-text (STT) transcribes input, DSPy modules handle reasoning and response generation, and text-to-speech (TTS) synthesizes output. DSPy signatures define structured LM calls (e.g., 'question -> answer') optimized via teleprompters like BootstrapFewShot. Latency budgets require <300ms end-to-end, with DSPy modules wrapped for verification.

DSPy programs chain modules like ChainOfThought or ReAct for conversational reasoning, but composition can produce unexpected outputs. Vex verifies response appropriateness, coherence across turns, and safety before TTS. STT/TTS use low-latency models like Whisper-Tiny and Piper TTS.
architectureOverview.diagram:
Audio Input → [STT: Whisper] → [DSPy Reasoning: CoT/ReAct] → [Vex Verify] → [TTS: Piper] → Audio Output
                  ↓
             [Latency: <300ms]    [Safety/Coherence Checks]

risks.name: Optimized prompts overfitting
risks.description: DSPy teleprompters generate prompts that memorize training examples but fail on novel voice queries
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies generalization with diverse test cases at inference time

risks.name: Chain-of-thought logic errors
risks.description: CoT modules produce plausible reasoning steps leading to wrong conversational responses
risks.mitigation: Vex checks reasoning validity against conversation history assertions

risks.name: Latency budget violations
risks.description: DSPy module inference exceeds 300ms, breaking real-time voice interaction
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces strict timing assertions on forward() calls

risks.name: Safety filter bypasses
risks.description: Optimized modules generate harmful content slipping through basic filters
risks.mitigation: Vex applies multi-layer safety verification before TTS

implementation.title: DSPy Signature for Voice Reasoning
implementation.description: Define typed signatures for conversational reasoning with conversation history context.
implementation.code:
import dspy
class VoiceReasoner(dspy.Signature):
    """Respond appropriately to voice conversation."""
    conversation_history: str = dspy.InputField()
    user_query: str = dspy.InputField()
    appropriate_response: str = dspy.OutputField(desc='Safe, coherent response <100 words')

reason_module = dspy.ChainOfThought(VoiceReasoner)

implementation.title: Real-time Voice Pipeline
implementation.description: Orchestrate STT → DSPy → TTS with latency tracking using Whisper and Piper.
implementation.code:
import time
import dspy
from vosk import Model, KaldiRecognizer  # or Whisper

def voice_pipeline(audio_chunk, history=''):
    start = time.time()
    transcript = stt_transcribe(audio_chunk)  # <100ms
    pred = reason_module(conversation_history=history, user_query=transcript)
    response = pred.appropriate_response
    audio_out = tts_synthesize(response)  # <150ms
    latency = time.time() - start
    assert latency < 0.3, f'Latency violation: {latency}s'
    return audio_out, response

implementation.title: DSPy Optimization for Voice
implementation.description: BootstrapFewShot optimization with voice-specific examples for low-latency inference.
implementation.code:
from dspy.teleprompt import BootstrapFewShot

voice_examples = [
    dspy.Example(conversation_history='Hi', user_query='Weather?', appropriate_response='Sunny, 72°F').with_inputs('conversation_history', 'user_query')
]
teleprompter = BootstrapFewShot(metric=lambda ex, pred, trace: 1.0 if 'safe' in pred.appropriate_response else 0)
optimized_reasoner = teleprompter.compile(reason_module, trainset=voice_examples)

implementation.title: Conversation State Management
implementation.description: Maintain coherence across turns with DSPy example caching and history truncation.
implementation.code:
class VoiceAgent(dspy.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.reason = dspy.ChainOfThought(VoiceReasoner)
        self.history = []
    
    def forward(self, audio):
        transcript = stt(audio)
        context = '\n'.join(self.history[-3:])  # Last 3 turns
        pred = self.reason(conversation_history=context, user_query=transcript)
        self.history.append(f'User: {transcript} | Bot: {pred.appropriate_response}')
        return pred.appropriate_response
